answer:
Snowflake APIs support four primary authentication methods: key pair authentication, OAuth, programmatic access tokens (PAT), and workload identity federation [1].

For all methods, include the authentication credentials in the request header using the format Authorization: Bearer <token> [1][2]. You may optionally include the header X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type with values KEYPAIR_JWT, OAUTH, or PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN to specify the method explicitly [1][2]. For workload identity federation, use the header X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type: WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_FEDERATION [1].

Key pair authentication requires generating a JWT locally using a private key and a public key fingerprint [2]. OAuth and PAT use generated tokens provided by Snowflake, while workload identity federation uses tokens from your cloud provider [1][3].
